_readme: Edit this file then run: python3 deploy.py
_comment_namespace: Kubernetes namespace for all deployed resources. Use different values for prod vs dev.

cluster._comment_context: The kubectl context to use. Leave empty to use whatever kubectl is currently pointing at.
cluster._comment_auto_k3d: Set to true ONLY if you have no cluster at all and want deploy.py to create a local one using k3d (requires Docker).
cluster._comment_name: Used as the k3d cluster name when auto_create_k3d is true. Ignored otherwise.
cluster.name: forensics-operator

access._comment: The hostname you will type in your browser. Add it to /etc/hosts pointing to your cluster's ingress IP.
access._comment_tls: Optional: paths to your own TLS certificate and private key files. Leave both empty to auto-generate a self-signed certificate (valid for 10 years).

secrets._comment_jwt: Generate a strong secret with: python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"
secrets._comment_admin: Default admin credentials created on first boot. Change admin_password immediately.

auth._comment: Set auth_enabled to false to disable authentication (dev/trusted-LAN only).

resources._comment: Reduce elasticsearch_heap_mb to 256 on machines with less than 8 GB RAM.
resources._comment_api: API resources: increase for better performance with large file uploads
resources._comment_processor: Processor resources: increase for faster forensic analysis. For VERY large files (10GB+ disk images, memory dumps), increase to 32Gi+
resources._comment_minio: MinIO resources: critical for upload performance

images._comment: Leave registry empty to auto-load images directly into the cluster (works for k3s, k3d, minikube, kind, Docker Desktop). Set registry to push/pull via a registry, e.g. 'docker.io/youruser/' (trailing slash required).
